description: Keys derived from high-cardinality input create endless entries that evict useful data and crater the hit rate.
body: Failure mode: cache keys derived from high-cardinality or unbounded input (raw URLs, per-request ids) create endless distinct entries that evict useful data and crater the hit rate. Detection signals: hit rate falls as traffic grows; eviction rate approaches insertion rate; memory pinned at the limit with low reuse. Do not key on unbounded inputs. Safer fallback: normalize and bucket keys, cap cardinality by namespacing, and only cache values with genuine reuse.
